title: RunCorrelations
description: CLIENT-SAFE correlation analysis for one optimization run, used by the terminal-first analytics surface to explain which measures move together and which parameters move a measure. Reports the correlation method, the sample size it was computed over, measure-to-measure correlations (r, p_value, coarse strength), and parameter-to-measure effects (effect, coarse confidence). Statistics here are computed over the run's own measured aggregates; this contract carries no proprietary example-scoring signals or formulas.

run_id.description: Identifier of the optimization run this correlation analysis describes.

sample_size.description: Number of trials/observations the correlations were computed over (drives reliability).

x.description: Name of the first measure in the pair.

y.description: Name of the second measure in the pair.

strength.description: Coarse strength bucket for the correlation magnitude.

parameter_correlations.description: Effect of each tuned parameter on a measure.

effect.description: Signed effect size of the parameter on the measure.

confidence.description: Coarse confidence bucket for the reported effect.

warnings.description: Client-safe caveats about the correlations (e.g. small sample, non-linear relationship).
